rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof by presenting evidence showing that Defendant's conduct fell within the applicable standard of care. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. Whether surgery should have been performed immediately, whether Dr. Leichtling's decision to first try to stabilize the patient was negligent, and whether immediate surgery would have changed the outcome are not issues within common knowledge. Defendant's expert testifies that surgery could not be performed immediately and if surgery had been attempted without steps being taken to stabilize Plaintiff, Plaintiff would have died on the operating table. (Schultz Decl. Ex. B, Gardiner Decl. at 8, 10.) Plaintiff fails to submit any evidence, let alone any expert evidence, that would contradict this expert opinion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which is consistent with the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and compliant with CCP section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: R. R. VS. EL TRABAJO INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Edward Alkhasov'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 3, Edward Alkhasov's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Granted, in part. The deposition of Plaintiff shall take place on March 20, 2013 at 10:00 a.m. at a court reporter's office in the City and County of San Francisco to be selected by Mr. Alkhasov. The selected reporter must be a certified court reporter. Mr. Alkhasov must provide a certified court interpreter in English/Spanish. The interpreter must bring evidence of certification to the deposition for review by Plaintiff's counsel. The deposition notice shall not include any other document request, but merely the location of the deposition. Mr. Alkhasov must serve notice of the deposition to all parties by mail no later than March 5, 2013. Plaintiff need not produce her government issued identification or passport. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KARL B. NICHOLAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Jucicial Council Of California'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 23, Respondent Jucicial Council Of California's Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to state facts sufficient to show that Defendant Judicial Council has a ministerial duty to require Defendant VLSP to represent Plaintiff or to establish or fund any particular legal services program. Indeed, the applicable law makes clear that any such acts are discretionary and not subject to mandamus relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KARL B. NICHOLAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Volunteer Legal Services Program'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate Or Prohibition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 23, Respondent Volunteer Legal Services Program's Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate Or Prohibition The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to state facts sufficient to show that Defendant VLSP's legal services are a mandatory or ministerial act. The Sargent Shriver Civil Counsel Act, Government Code Section 68650-51, does not guarantee legal representation for civil harassment cases in general, let alone any particular case. rulings.Case Title: HBC ASSOCIATES LLC VS. LUCE FORWARD HAMILTON SCRIPPS, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Depositions Of Jeffrey Fillerup And William Magston; And Monetary Sanctions Against Mckenna, Long And Aldridge
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 1, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Depositions Of Jeffrey Fillerup And William Magston; And Monetary Sanctions Against Mckenna, Long And Aldridge. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear in light of pending motion for summary judgment set which was to be heard on February 15, 2013 or to notify court by sending an email to rkane1089@aol.com by 4 p.m. today that motion is moot. If the matter is not moot, the parties shall be prepared to respond specifically in the order set forth in Plaintiff's separate statement to each of the matters in dispute. In addition, the parties should be prepared to address the applicability of Rifkind v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 22 CA4th 1255, 1263 as to whether the questions at issue are factually or contentions, the effect that the work product privilege is held by the attorney (Wells Fargo v. Sup. Ct. (2000) 22 C.4th 201, 215), and the effect, if any, of the ruling on the summary judgment motion. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT P. LAROCCA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF VS. LEO J. LAROCCA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT LEO LAROCCA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The motions are denied. A moving party's unilateral, unexpressed hope that another sibling will take care of the legal obligations arising from the lawsuit is not excusable neglect, and, in any event, failure to act on the default for several months is not appropriate diligence, and participation in a mediation is not a substitute for a responsive pleading. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RYAN GARMAN et al VS. UNIVERSITY PIPELINE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANTS TOM UNGER, STEPHANIE LOSEE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained, without leave to amend as to the fifth, and with leave to amend as to the sixth and seventh causes of action, and overruled as to the remaining causes of action and alter ego allegations. Given the requirement to accept as true the pleaded facts, no matter how improbable, Plaintiffs have sufficiently pleaded alter ego. For the first, second and third causes of action, Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled facts to allege breach of contract and Labor Code violations claims under the alter ego doctrine. "The essence of the alter ego doctrine is not that the individual shareholder becomes the corporation, but that the individual shareholder is liable for the actions of the corporation." (Leek v. Cooper (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 399, 415.) For the fifth cause of action, Mr. Unger cannot conspire with University Pipeline, Inc. where alter ego liability is alleged. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 512, fn. 4.) Even alternatively, he could not conspire with his employer. For the sixth cause of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to allege conversion of property other than unpaid wages. (Thomas v. Home Depot USA Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2007) 527 F.Supp.2d 1003, 1010.) For the seventh cause of action, though Plaintiffs may plead inconsistent legal theories, Plaintiff does not plead the nonexistence or nonenforceability of the employment agreement. (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1389-90.) The pleadings are not uncertain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash The Subpoena To The Custodian Of Records Of Mega Bank, Wing Lung Bank, Lone Oak Fund Llc And Preferred Bank Or In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY Notice Of Motion To Quash The Subpoena To The Custodian Of Records Of Mega Bank, Wing Lung Bank, Lone Oak Fund Llc And Preferred Bank Or In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The category of documents requested in the subpoenas at issue are overbroad. The parties are to meet and confer prior to the hearing regarding a more limited scope of records and protective order as to each subpoena and to the extent no agreement can be reached the parties are to address the issue of relevance of the more limited scope as to each of the subpoeaed parties to the extent that there are different relevance issues. Any records to be produced will be produced pursuant to a protective order to which the parties shall meet and confer as to its terms. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THERABOTANICS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. SOLAZYME, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTS FREDERIC STOECKEL, SOLAZYME, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend as to all of the causes of action against Mr. Stoeckel. Plaintiffs have leave to plead facts showing that Mr. Stoeckel participated in, directed, or authorized the wrongdoing. The Court overrules Solazyme's demurer to the declaratory relief cause of action because plaintiff seeks prospective relief. The Court sustains the demurrers to the 5th, 6th, and 7th causes of action for plaintiff to plead these fraud claims with specificity. The demurrer to the conversion cause of action is overruled. The Court sustains with 15 days leave to amend the demurrer to the UCL cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to plead the unlawful or fraudulent conduct with more particularity. The Court rejects defendant's other arguments on the UCL cause of action. The Court recognizes that there is no distinct cause of action for an injunction, unjust enrichment, or constructive trust. Under CCP 452, the Court views the allegations as general charging allegations or a remedy request and overrules. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: THERABOTANICS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. SOLAZYME, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT SEPHORA USA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The Court overrules the demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action because plaintiff seeks prospective relief. The Court sustains with 15 days leave to amend the demurrer to the UCL cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to plead the unlawful or fraudulent conduct with more particularity. The Court rejects defendant's other arguments on the UCL cause of action. The Court overrules as to the two interference causes of action. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges knowledge and wrongful conduct that is distinct from the interference itself. The Court recognizes that there is no distinct cause of action for an injunction, unjust enrichment, or constructive trust. Under CCP 452, the Court views the allegations as general charging allegations or a remedy request and overrules. The Court overrules as to the accounting cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THE BAUGHMAN COMPANY INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOACHIM BERGMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2013, Line 21, Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction Granted in part. Defendants are enjoined from retaining or using and ordered to destroy all of Plaintiff's files and documents, electronic or otherwise, which Defendants took with them when they left the Plaintiff. The remainder of the Order to Show Cause is denied. Plaintiff has not shown as to other issues either that it is likely to prevail on the merits, that it lacks a remedy at law, that there is a danger of injury not already incurred, or that the balance of harms tips in its favor. Plaintiff is required to post a bond in the amount of $15,000.
